narrative:
Skies are partly to mostly sunny with a warmer afternoon. The chance for a late-day pop-up shower is minuscule but non-zero, and present from Watauga southward. On Friday, there will be a little bit better coverage of afternoon and evening showers and thunderstorms. Hit-or-miss action continues over the weekend for the PM-periods. Next week, opportunities for rainfall dwindle as heat begins to build.

Sunshine, Warming-Up

Last Updated Thu June 25 at 4:13PM by Scott Stephens

Skies are partly to mostly sunny with a warmer afternoon. The chance for a late-day pop-up shower is minuscule but non-zero, and present from Watauga southward. On Friday, there will be a little bit better coverage of afternoon and evening showers and thunderstorms. Hit-or-miss action continues over the weekend for the PM-periods. Next week, opportunities for rainfall dwindle as heat begins to build.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Pre-Publication Sale is Underway!

The 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar is being printed now and will be available around July 1. Until then, the Pre-Publication Sale is underway with the best price we will offer all year. Get your 2027 calendars at 26% off when you buy 2 or more. As in previous years, we have scheduled special Pickup times around NW NC to save you the cost of shipping. Forecast Discussion

The western extension of the Bermuda High will be overhead today, providing generally pleasant weather with a warmer afternoon.

On Friday, an Ohio Valley cold front gets close enough to encourage a few afternoon and evening showers and thunderstorms to develop.

This front makes it into Virginia over the weekend, before getting repelled to the north by early next week. As a result, we will maintain widely-scattered PM showers and thunderstorms both weekend days.

By next week, a large dome of high pressure will be building just to our west. This heat dome will crank up our temperatures, while also restricting our rainfall potential to just isolated heat-of-the-day thundershower developments.

Rather hot and mostly dry weather will continue in the run-up to the Fourth of July.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Monday Message for June 22, 2026

The week starts stormy, but ends up like the first full week of summer should, with hit-or-miss afternoon thundershowers and very warm temperatures. It's the Monday Message, available for our subscribers.

Jun 22, 2026
Yadkin Valley Deluge

A morning complex of thunderstorms moving across the Ohio Valley and into West Virginia and Virginia produced an outflow boundary that pushed southward into North Carolina during the afternoon of June 18. This outflow boundary, the result of a cool rush of air from thunderstorms occurring to the north, served as an effective focusing mechanism for training thunderstorms that occurred over parts of Surry, northern Wilkes and southern Alleghany County during Thursday afternoon and evening.

This occurred under the backdrop of increasing tropical moisture returning from the southwest as the remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur moved into Alabama.

The result was excessive rainfall, in the range of 2-4 inches, with locally higher amounts of around 5 inches in central Surry County.
